summary: The Sites Project Authority (Sites Authority), a California joint exercise of powers authority governing the Sites Reservoir Project, is soliciting comprehensive proposals for the acquisition and implementation of a cloud-based accounting and financial reporting software system to replace its current QuickBooks Desktop Premier 2024 platform. The core ERP scope encompasses Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, Bank Reconciliation, Budget Management, Cash Management, Fixed Assets, General Ledger, Project Accounting, Purchasing, and General & Technical modules, with additional optional functionality available for capital asset maintenance management, debt management, investment management, human resources, payroll, timekeeping, and inventory tracking. The contract is structured with an initial one-year term and up to two optional five-year extensions at the Authority's sole discretion, for a maximum potential term of eleven years, with a target go-live date of January 1, 2027 and pricing required to remain firm for at least three years from contract execution with subsequent annual increases capped at CPI-U or three percent, whichever is less. This procurement is strategically significant as the Sites Authority anticipates transitioning from planning to final design, construction, and long-term operations of a 1.5 million acre-foot off-stream surface water storage reservoir—California's planned 8th largest reservoir—with expected significant growth in transaction volume, contract complexity, debt financing, grant management, and reporting requirements. The selected ERP system must be modular, extensible, and capable of scaling to support the project through construction completion targeted by end of 2033, serving as the Authority's core financial system of record over a long-term horizon. Proposals are evaluated on a best-value basis across software capabilities (30%), technical approach (25%), pricing (20%), project goals response (10%), firm qualifications (10%), and integration scalability (5%), with the system expected to support approximately 5-10 ERP users and 10-20 workflow approvers currently, and data migration from one completed fiscal year of QuickBooks transactions.
periodOfPerformance: 1 year base term with up to two 5-year optional extensions (maximum 11 years total)
title: Financial Enterprise Resource Planning Software and Services
placeOfPerformance: Glenn and Colusa Counties, California (Sites Reservoir Project)
